findings.title: Generic OG Image Alt Text Across All Pages
findings.description: All pages use the same generic alt text for Open Graph images: '${site.productName} social preview'. This generic text doesn't describe the specific content of each page, reducing the effectiveness of social sharing and missing opportunities for contextual information in social media platforms and apps that display OG metadata.

findings.title: Missing JSON-LD Schema on Gallery Frontend Pages
findings.description: The frontend gallery application (frontend/gallery.html, frontend/accessibility.html, etc.) has basic meta tags (title, description, OG tags) but no JSON-LD structured data. This prevents search engines and AI models from understanding the semantic structure of gallery pages, missing opportunities for rich snippets and AI discoverability.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add JSON-LD WebPage schema to frontend/gallery.html after the Twitter Card meta tags:

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "WebPage",
  "name": "Client Photo Gallery",
  "description": "View and download your photo gallery. Powered by Analogify Studio and ChromaHaus automated image processing.",
  "url": "https://galleries.analogifystudio.com/gallery.html",
  "publisher": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Analogify Studio",
    "logo": "https://analogifystudio.com/og-default.svg"
  },
  "about": {
    "@type": "Thing",
    "name": "Photo Gallery",
    "description": "Secure, branded photo gallery with selective downloads and moderated comments"
  },
  "isAccessibleForFree": false
}
</script>

findings.title: FAQ Schema Missing on Zen FAQ Section
findings.description: The Analogify Zen page (zen.astro, lines 218-240) has a well-structured FAQ section with questions and answers, but it lacks FAQPage and Question schema markup. This misses the opportunity for featured snippets in search results and voice assistant integration.

findings.title: Inconsistent Meta Description Length
findings.description: Meta descriptions vary significantly in length. Most are 120-160 characters (optimal), but some are shorter, like accessibility.astro at 47 characters. While Google will generate its own if needed, shorter descriptions reduce SERP click-through rate and appear truncated.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Expand meta description to 120-160 characters:

Change from:
description="Accessibility is ongoing engineering work."

To:
description="Learn about Analogify's ongoing commitment to accessibility, WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, and how we build inclusive photo gallery experiences for all photographers."

findings.title: Suboptimal Meta Descriptions on Support and Security Pages
findings.description: Support and security pages have descriptions under 100 characters. These pages are likely search targets for photographers looking for help or trust signals, and shorter descriptions reduce CTR.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Expand descriptions to 120-160 characters:

Support page:
Current: "Support for workflow questions, access issues, and delivery problems."
Suggested: "Get help with Analogify Studio workflows, access issues, gallery management, and delivery questions. Response time: 2 business days. Privacy-first support process."

Security page:
Current: "Built around controlled delivery, not public-by-default media sharing."
Suggested: "Analogify Studio prioritizes photographer privacy and client security. Learn about our controlled-delivery architecture, encrypted downloads, moderated comments, and privacy-first approach to gallery platforms."

findings.title: Missing BreadcrumbList Schema on Multi-Level Content
findings.description: For a flat-structure site, breadcrumbs aren't critical, but pages like 'Demo' and 'Features' could benefit from BreadcrumbList schema to show hierarchy in search results (Home > Features or Home > Demo).

findings.title: No Image Optimization Indicators on Frontend App
findings.description: Frontend gallery application (frontend/gallery.html) likely serves user-uploaded photos but lacks visible optimization patterns like lazy loading attributes, srcset, or WebP format hints. This is likely handled by JavaScript framework, but best practices suggest explicit attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: If frontend gallery serves images dynamically via JavaScript, document the optimization in comments or verify the JS framework is: 1. Using lazy loading (loading="lazy" attribute or equivalent) 2. Serving responsive srcset (multiple resolutions) 3. Using modern formats with fallbacks (WebP/AVIF with JPEG fallback) 4. Setting explicit width/height to prevent layout shift Example pattern for image rendering in generated HTML:

findings.title: Consider IndexNow Integration for Faster Indexing
findings.description: The site has a valid sitemap and robots.txt, but IndexNow (Microsoft Bing's fast indexing API) is not integrated. This allows instant notification to search engines when new content is published.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Optional future enhancement:
1. Generate IndexNow key from Bing Webmaster Tools
2. Add to public/index-now-key.txt
3. Call IndexNow API on build:
   POST https://api.indexnow.org/indexnow
   Body: { "host": "analogifystudio.com", "key": "...", "urlList": [...] }
4. Or add to sitemap header in robots.txt for automatic pickup

technical_seo.notes: Excellent technical foundation with proper meta tags, robots.txt, sitemaps, and semantic HTML

ai_optimization.notes: Good AI-readiness but missing FAQ schema on FAQ content and JSON-LD on gallery pages

content_seo.notes: Strong content structure with proper headings, but some meta descriptions are shorter than optimal

performance_seo.notes: Good performance signals with async scripts and Astro static generation

social_meta.notes: Complete OG/Twitter implementation but image alt text is generic across all pages

findings.title: Missing robots.txt file
findings.description: No robots.txt file exists at the web root. Required to guide search engine crawlers and control indexing behavior.
findings.fix_suggestion: Create /robots.txt at web root with standard rules for crawlers and sitemap reference.

findings.title: Missing sitemap.xml file
findings.description: No sitemap.xml exists to list all public pages. Critical for search engine discovery.
findings.fix_suggestion: Generate sitemap.xml listing all 24 public dashboard pages with lastmod dates and priorities.

findings.title: Missing meta descriptions on 22 of 24 dashboard pages
findings.description: Only admin.html and self-audit.html have meta descriptions. Missing on all other pages. Meta descriptions appear in search results and affect click-through rates.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add unique 120-160 character meta descriptions to each dashboard HTML file explaining page purpose and content.

findings.title: Missing canonical URLs on all dashboard pages
findings.description: No canonical link elements found on any dashboard. Canonicals prevent duplicate content issues and consolidate link equity.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add self-referencing canonical links to each dashboard page pointing to correct absolute URLs.

findings.title: Missing Open Graph meta tags
findings.description: No Open Graph tags found. OG tags enable rich previews when pages are shared on social platforms.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type to each page. Create 1200x630px OG images.

findings.title: Missing Twitter Card meta tags
findings.description: No Twitter Card tags found. Enables rich previews on X when links are shared.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image meta tags. Use 1200x675px images.

findings.title: No structured data (JSON-LD) markup
findings.description: No schema.org JSON-LD found on dashboard pages. Important for AI search engines and voice assistants.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add Organization and WebSite schema to base template. Add department-specific schema to relevant pages.

findings.title: Inconsistent page title patterns
findings.description: Titles follow three different patterns: some with brand, some without. Inconsistency reduces SEO impact.
findings.fix_suggestion: Standardize all titles to consistent format: [Page Name] — Cody Jo Method (50-60 characters).

findings.title: H1 heading structure varies across pages
findings.description: H1 placement and structure inconsistent. Best practice is one H1 per page in prominent location.
findings.fix_suggestion: Ensure every page has exactly one H1 as main heading. Keep semantic structure clean.

findings.title: Content rendered by JavaScript not visible to crawlers
findings.description: Key dashboard data rendered via JavaScript into empty div containers. AI crawlers may not see dynamically-rendered content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider server-side rendering for key sections or ensure JavaScript content is crawlable.

findings.title: Minimal static content optimized for organic search
findings.description: Dashboard pages are 95% CSS/JavaScript with little static text. No landing page with marketing copy for organic search.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add landing page with static content explaining Back Office purpose. Ensure documentation pages have crawlable content.

findings.title: Missing image alt attributes
findings.description: Dynamic images (e.g., product thumbnails) lack alt text. Static images may also lack alt attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add descriptive alt text to all img tags. Use alt='' for decorative images.

findings.title: Link handling could be optimized
findings.description: Internal links appropriately avoid target='_blank'. External links would benefit from rel attributes.
findings.fix_suggestion: For future external links, use rel='noopener noreferrer' for security.

findings.title: JavaScript loading could be optimized
findings.description: Scripts loaded synchronously in head. Could defer non-critical scripts to improve LCP.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add defer attribute to non-critical scripts or move to end of body.

findings.title: Google Fonts loading already optimized
findings.description: Fonts already use display=swap for optimal loading. No action needed.
findings.fix_suggestion: No action needed. Font loading is properly optimized.

findings.title: FAQ schema underutilized
findings.description: FAQ page has Q&A content but lacks FAQPage schema markup for AI assistants and voice search.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add FAQPage schema with Question/Answer pairs extracted from faq.html content.

findings.title: No breadcrumb schema for navigation
findings.description: Pages lack BreadcrumbList schema to indicate navigation hierarchy.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add BreadcrumbList schema to main dashboard pages (optional).

findings.title: Product-specific pages lack unique content
findings.description: Product dashboards (selah.html, analogify.html, etc.) are duplicates of index.html. No unique descriptions or content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add unique descriptions and brief product overviews to each dashboard.

findings.title: 18 exported routes reuse the homepage title, description, and canonical URL
findings.description: The root metadata in the App Router layout defines homepage-level title, description, Open Graph, Twitter, and URL values, but most routes do not override them. In the generated export, 18 route HTML files reuse the same title, the same 148-character meta description, and the same canonical URL pointing to https://selah.codyjo.com/. That collapses page relevance, creates duplicate metadata across the site, and tells search engines that non-home pages canonicalize back to the homepage.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add route-level `metadata` or `generateMetadata()` exports for every public page so each route emits a unique title, description, canonical URL, and OG/Twitter URL. Example: `export const metadata = { title: "Reading Plans", description: "Browse guided Bible reading plans...", alternates: { canonical: "https://selah.codyjo.com/plans/" }, openGraph: { url: "https://selah.codyjo.com/plans/" } }`.

findings.title: Books and Study export as client-side placeholders instead of crawlable content
findings.description: Two SEO-critical routes, `/books/` and `/study/`, do not ship meaningful HTML in the static export. The generated HTML contains a client-render bailout template and loading copy instead of headings, navigation, or text content. Search engines and AI systems that rely on the initial HTML only see placeholders until JavaScript finishes, which is especially risky for a static-export site.
findings.fix_suggestion: Move the route shell and default content into server-rendered App Router components, and avoid forcing `useSearchParams()` bailouts for the primary page body. Pre-render a static browse experience with headings and internal links, then hydrate interactive chapter loading on top.

findings.title: The sitemap omits five public indexable routes
findings.description: The codebase exports 17 page routes from `src/app`, but `public/sitemap.xml` includes only 12 URLs. The missing routes are `/updates`, `/login`, `/register`, `/forgot-password`, and `/reset-password`. All five pages are publicly reachable and currently emit `index, follow` robots tags, so the sitemap is incomplete relative to the live route set.
findings.fix_suggestion: Generate the sitemap from the route manifest or maintain it from a single source of truth so every public route is emitted automatically. If auth pages should not be indexed, change their robots directives to `noindex` instead of leaving them public but absent from the sitemap.

findings.title: The sitemap has no `lastmod` dates for any URL
findings.description: The sitemap lists `loc`, `changefreq`, and `priority`, but it never emits `lastmod`. That removes an explicit freshness signal for search engines and makes the static sitemap harder to trust for recently updated routes such as `/updates/` and content-like pages.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add `<lastmod>` for every URL, preferably from build metadata or content timestamps. Example: `<lastmod>2026-03-11</lastmod>`.

findings.title: 404 pages send conflicting robots directives and canonicalize to the homepage
findings.description: The generated 404 output includes both `noindex` and `index, follow`, and also emits a canonical pointing at the homepage. That creates mixed signals for crawlers: the page says it is a 404, tells bots not to index it, then also inherits the homepage canonical and global metadata.
findings.fix_suggestion: Override metadata for the not-found route so it emits only `noindex, follow` and no homepage canonical. In Next App Router, set a dedicated `metadata` object for the not-found boundary rather than inheriting the root canonical.

findings.title: The updates page is orphaned in crawlable HTML
findings.description: The only product navigation link to `/updates` lives inside a menu that renders only when `menuOpen` is true. In the exported HTML, that menu is closed by default, so crawlers do not see a normal internal link to `/updates/`. Combined with the sitemap omission, the updates page has weak discoverability.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add a normal static link to `/updates` in always-rendered navigation, the footer, or the homepage, and include the route in the sitemap.

findings.title: The updates feed lacks semantic article/time markup and freshness schema
findings.description: The updates page renders change-log entries with generic `div` and `span` elements. It does not use `<article>`, `<time dateTime>`, or `BlogPosting`/`Article` JSON-LD, even though the underlying data includes publication dates and titles. That leaves AI systems with weaker entity, date, and freshness cues.
findings.fix_suggestion: Render each entry as an `<article>` with a `<time dateTime={update.date}>` element, and add `BlogPosting` JSON-LD containing `headline`, `datePublished`, `dateModified`, and `description`.

findings.title: Open Graph and Twitter tags on non-home pages still point to the homepage
findings.description: The layout hard-codes homepage Open Graph and Twitter values, and most routes never override them. In the exported HTML, non-home pages such as `/updates/`, `/books/`, `/journal/`, and `/study/` all share the homepage OG title, description, and URL. Shared links therefore preview as the homepage instead of the page being shared.
findings.fix_suggestion: Set route-specific `openGraph` and `twitter` metadata per page, including page-specific `title`, `description`, and `url`. For example, `/updates/` should emit `og:url=https://selah.codyjo.com/updates/` and its own release-note title/description.

findings.title: Built pages do not emit `og:image` or `twitter:image` tags
findings.description: Although the root metadata object defines image values, the generated HTML pages do not include `og:image` or `twitter:image`. The build output shows title, description, URL, site name, and card tags only. As a result, shared URLs may render without a predictable preview image.
findings.fix_suggestion: Verify the metadata build emits image tags for exported routes, preferably using an absolute PNG or JPG URL such as `https://selah.codyjo.com/og.png`, then confirm `og:image` and `twitter:image` are present in the built HTML.

findings.title: Primary content on key routes is delayed behind hydration and placeholder UI
findings.description: Key routes rely on client-side state and hydration before meaningful content appears. The exported HTML for `/books/`, `/study/`, `/login/`, `/register/`, and `/reset-password/` is a spinner or loading message rather than the final page body, while the document still loads multiple JavaScript chunks up front. That pattern raises the cost of first render and delays meaningful paint.
findings.fix_suggestion: Server-render the initial headings, descriptive copy, and navigation for critical routes, then hydrate only the interactive controls. That reduces main-thread work and gives crawlers and users useful HTML immediately.

findings.title: Noindex Back Office routes are still published in the sitemap
findings.description: The generated sitemap includes Back Office admin/demo/dashboard-launch routes that explicitly send `noindex, nofollow`. That creates conflicting crawl signals: the sitemap asks crawlers to discover the URLs while the page-level robots tag asks them not to index them.
findings.fix_suggestion: Exclude all `noindex` routes from sitemap generation. In Astro, either move those routes out of the public sitemap path, or filter them out when generating the sitemap so only canonical indexable URLs are emitted.

findings.title: The public documentation endpoint is missing a canonical URL
findings.description: The `/back-office/documentation.html` route is generated as raw HTML instead of using the shared `Base` layout, and it omits a canonical tag entirely.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add `<link rel="canonical" href="https://codyjo.com/back-office/documentation.html">` to the head, or migrate the page into an Astro page that reuses `Base.astro` so canonical generation stays consistent site-wide.

findings.title: The public documentation page ships with no JSON-LD or entity schema
findings.description: The same `/back-office/documentation.html` endpoint lacks structured data entirely, so search engines and AI systems only see untyped HTML rather than explicit documentation, organization, or article entities.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add JSON-LD for `TechArticle` or `Article` plus publisher/organization metadata, or move the page onto the shared layout and pass a page-specific schema object.

findings.title: The public documentation page has no Open Graph or Twitter Card tags
findings.description: Because `/back-office/documentation.html` bypasses `Base.astro`, it also misses the site-wide social meta implementation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:url`, `og:image`, `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, and `twitter:image`, or migrate the page to the shared layout.

findings.title: All sitemap lastmod values are set to build time instead of page freshness
findings.description: The Astro sitemap integration is configured with `lastmod: new Date()`, so every URL in the sitemap gets the same timestamp from the build rather than an actual content modification date.
findings.fix_suggestion: Remove the global build-time `lastmod`, or compute per-page dates from content frontmatter or git timestamps so blog posts and static pages emit meaningful freshness data.

findings.title: Flagship product pages only expose generic WebSite schema
findings.description: Product landing pages such as Analogify, ChromaHaus, Selah, and SEO Privacy Tracker call `Base` without a page-specific schema, so the layout falls back to a generic `WebSite` entity instead of `SoftwareApplication`, `Product`, or another product-specific type.
findings.fix_suggestion: Pass page-level JSON-LD to each product page using `SoftwareApplication` or `Product` with name, description, URL, applicationCategory, audience, and publisher fields.

findings.title: The blog article title template produces titles far beyond recommended length
findings.description: Blog post pages append the full product blog name to every article title. That pushes many generated titles into the 85-119 character range, well beyond the requested 50-60 character target.
findings.fix_suggestion: Shorten the template, for example by dropping the blog suffix for posts or using a shorter brand suffix such as `| Cody Jo Method` only when needed.

findings.title: Product blog hub metadata is consistently out of range
findings.description: The product blog index template creates short titles and very long descriptions across all six product hubs. Generated descriptions land between 227 and 279 characters, and some titles fall well below the 50-60 character target.
findings.fix_suggestion: Rewrite the template to produce focused 50-60 character titles and 120-160 character descriptions per product hub, keeping the core keyword and audience but removing the extra explanatory sentence fragments.

findings.title: Missing meta description
findings.description: The page is missing a meta description tag. While this is an internal tool, a description improves browser tab clarity and API documentation.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add: <meta name="description" content="Configure and deploy Puppet Enterprise demo environments on GCP. Single-page application for infrastructure provisioning.">

findings.title: Missing robots meta tag
findings.description: No robots meta tag present. For an authenticated internal tool, should explicitly prevent indexing.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add: <meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow, noarchive">

findings.title: Missing Open Graph tags
findings.description: No OG meta tags for sharing links. Even internal tools benefit from proper link previews.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add OG tags:
<meta property="og:title" content="PE Demo Bootstrap">
<meta property="og:description" content="Configure and deploy Puppet Enterprise demo environments on GCP.">
<meta property="og:type" content="website">

findings.title: Missing H1 heading
findings.description: The page has no H1 tag. Found 3 H2s and 1 H3s. Best practice: exactly one H1 per page.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add H1 at top of main:

PE Demo Bootstrap

Change first

to follow proper hierarchy.

findings.title: Limited semantic HTML structure
findings.description: The page uses divs where semantic elements could improve structure: missing aside element for supplementary content.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add <footer> at end. Consider using <article> for form sections. Current structure is acceptable for a form-heavy app.

findings.title: No images on page
findings.description: This form-based interface has no images. This is acceptable for a data entry tool.
findings.fix_suggestion: Not applicable.

findings.title: No JSON-LD structured data
findings.description: The page has no JSON-LD markup. For an internal tool, this is optional but could help with API documentation and automation.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add SoftwareApplication schema:
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "SoftwareApplication",
  "name": "PE Demo Bootstrap",
  "description": "Infrastructure provisioning tool for Puppet Enterprise on GCP"
}
</script>

findings.title: External CSS render-blocking
findings.description: Stylesheet is loaded synchronously in head. For a SPA, could improve LCP by inlining critical styles.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider: 1) Move CSS to end of body, 2) Use async CSS loading, or 3) Inline critical CSS in head.

findings.title: Missing security headers (CSP)
findings.description: No Content-Security-Policy meta tag. For authenticated internal tools, CSP improves security.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add: <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://accounts.google.com/gsi/client; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';">

findings.title: External authentication script
findings.description: Google Sign-In script is loaded asynchronously (good). Consider fetchpriority for auth-critical load.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider adding: <script src="..." async defer fetchpriority="high"></script> if this is critical path.

findings.title: Links using href='#' with onclick handlers
findings.description: Found 3 anchor tags using href='#' with onclick. These don't work without JavaScript.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use

findings.title: Heavy use of event handlers on non-button elements
findings.description: Found 10 onclick handlers. 1 are on divs/non-button elements.
findings.fix_suggestion: Use <button type="button" onclick="..."> instead of <div onclick="...">. Current buttons are correct; ensure all clickable elements are <button> or <a>.

findings.title: Page title is concise (potentially too short)
findings.description: Title is "PE Demo Bootstrap" (17 chars). Optimal: 50-60 characters for better SERP display.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change to: <title>PE Demo Bootstrap - Configure Puppet Enterprise on GCP</title>

findings.title: Missing metadata on 46 non-marketing pages
findings.description: 46 of 67 pages lack exported metadata objects, including critical user-facing pages: /login, /register, /reset-password, /forgot-password, /create, /reader, /study, /journal, /admin, /settings, /quick-start, and all /e/[slug]/* dynamic routes. Without metadata, these pages have missing or default titles, descriptions, and OG tags.

findings.title: Sitemap missing 46 pages
findings.description: The static sitemap.xml includes only 18 public marketing pages but excludes 46 pages: auth flows (/login, /register, /reset-password), organizer tools (/create, /admin), user utilities (/settings, /reader, /study), and all dynamic event routes (/e/[slug] and children). Sitemaps should include all crawlable pages.

findings.title: Missing viewport meta tag explicit declaration
findings.description: Root layout.tsx does not explicitly declare a viewport meta tag. While Next.js provides a default viewport, best practice is to explicitly declare it for clarity and SEO crawlers.

findings.title: Dynamic pages missing canonical URLs
findings.description: Dynamic /e/[slug]/* pages (46 pages total) lack canonical URL declarations. For a static export build, canonical URLs should explicitly point to the published URL to prevent indexing issues with query parameters or protocol mismatches.

findings.title: Missing OpenGraph and Twitter card metadata on 46 pages
findings.description: 46 pages lack openGraph and twitter metadata objects. Marketing pages (15 pages) have both, but user-facing pages (/login, /create, /settings, /reader, /e/[slug]/*, etc.) default to root layout OG tags instead of page-specific social sharing metadata.

findings.title: No structured data for Article, Event, or FAQPage schema
findings.description: Codebase includes WebApplication and Organization schema (root layout) but no Article, Event, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, or Person schema. Event pages and feature pages could benefit from rich snippet markup.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add structured data to key page types:

1. Event pages (/e/[slug]/): Add Event schema with name, description, startDate, endDate, location, image.
2. Features pages: Add Article schema with headline, description, datePublished, author.
3. FAQ content: Use FAQPage schema if applicable.

Example Event schema for /e/[slug]/:

script type="application/ld+json"
{
  '@context': 'https://schema.org',
  '@type': 'Event',
  name: event.name,
  description: event.description,
  startDate: event.startDate,
  endDate: event.endDate,
  location: { '@type': 'Place', name: event.venue },
  image: event.ogImage,
  url: absoluteUrl(`/e/${event.slug}/`)
}

findings.title: Images missing width/height attributes on some components
findings.description: Most Next.js Image components have width/height (e.g., directory QR codes: width={80}, height={80}), but the contact page uses a regular tag without width/height attributes: with no dimensions, causing potential Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).

findings.title: No lazy loading attribute on above-fold images
findings.description: Images in components use loading="eager" or default to eager (non-lazy) loading. The contact page photo uses loading="eager" even though it's above the fold, which is correct. However, many images below the fold should have loading="lazy" to improve page load performance and LCP.
findings.fix_suggestion: Audit all tags and Image components for lazy loading: - Above-fold images (hero, header): loading="eager" or default (no lazy) - Below-fold images (gallery, testimonials): loading="lazy" - Next.js Image: use priority={true} for LCP images, default lazy for others

findings.title: Inconsistent heading hierarchy on pricing page
findings.description: Pricing page uses single <h1> at line 84 but content flows from context div -> h1 with various <h2> elements and styled text. No H2 headings wrapping major sections (packages, included features, next steps), reducing semantic structure for screen readers and AI models.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add semantic H2 wrappers for major content sections:

Straightforward pilot packages...

Choose Your Package

{packages.map(pkg =>

{pkg.name}

)}

What's Included in Every Pilot

{/* included features list */}

findings.title: Limited content structure optimization for AI extraction
findings.description: Homepage and feature pages use prose blocks and feature cards but don't employ clear 'answer upfront' patterns or bullet lists that AI models and featured snippet algorithms prefer. Content layout is marketing-oriented (carousel, gradient sections) rather than extraction-optimized (question-answer, lists, direct answers).
findings.fix_suggestion:
Add FAQ schema and structured content sections: 1. Add explicit 'What is Cordivent?' answer in first 50 words of homepage 2. Add FAQ section to /features with structured Q&A pairs (Who is it for? What makes it different? How does it work?) 3. Implement FAQPage JSON-LD schema 4. Break feature descriptions into bullet lists with numbered steps (How it works section already exists, make it more extraction-friendly) Example:

Common Questions

  • What is Cordivent?

    Cordivent is...

findings.title: OG image is static SVG, no per-page dynamic images
findings.description: All pages (root + marketing pages that have OG tags) use the same static og.svg image. Events, user content (gallery), and directory don't have dynamic OG images generated from event data.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Implement dynamic OG image generation for event pages:

1. Use library like `@vercel/og` or canvas-based image generation
2. Create event-specific OG image endpoint: /api/og?slug=event-slug
3. Reference in generateMetadata():

images: [{ 
  url: `${SITE_URL_NO_TRAILING_SLASH}/api/og?slug=${event.slug}`,
  width: 1200, 
  height: 630,
  alt: event.name
}]

findings.title: robots.txt hardcodes cordivent.com domain
findings.description: robots.txt includes hardcoded sitemap reference: 'Sitemap: https://cordivent.com/sitemap.xml'. If deployed to staging/preview domains, crawlers request sitemap from production domain instead of the current host.

findings.title: OG images use static SVG instead of recommended formats
findings.description: og.svg (1.1KB) is a static SVG. While valid, PNG or JPEG would be more widely supported across social platforms. SVG on some platforms may not render or may render incorrectly.
findings.fix_suggestion: Convert static og.svg to og.png or og.jpg (ensure 1200x630px). If dynamic OG images are later implemented, use PNG/JPEG output.

findings.title: Missing BreadcrumbList schema
findings.description: Multi-level pages (/e/[slug]/agenda, /e/[slug]/manage/guests, etc.) lack BreadcrumbList JSON-LD schema. Breadcrumbs help search engines understand site hierarchy and improve navigation in search results.

findings.title: Twitter Card uses summary_large_image but no image dimensions specified
findings.description: Twitter metadata in layout sets card type 'summary_large_image' but Twitter Card specification recommends explicit dimensions in alt text or image metadata. Current implementation: twitter: { images: [{ url: '/og.svg', alt: '...' }] } lacks width/height.

findings.title: No alt text for emoji icons used as images
findings.description: Login and register pages use emoji with role="img" and aria-label (e.g., ✍️). While semantically correct for a11y, emoji as content decoration should use role="presentation" or img with alt="" if decorative.
findings.fix_suggestion: If emoji is purely decorative, use role="presentation" instead: ✍️ If it's content, current approach (role="img" + aria-label) is correct.

findings.title: Static export prevents dynamic sitemap generation
findings.description: With output: 'export' in next.config.ts, the Next.js sitemap() function cannot be used (it requires dynamic server-side rendering). Current sitemap.xml is static and must be manually updated as pages are added.
findings.fix_suggestion:
If pages are added frequently, consider build-time sitemap generation:

1. Create build script that generates sitemap.xml at build time from route map
2. Or: accept manual sitemap.xml updates when adding pages
3. Or: switch to hybrid rendering (output: 'standalone') with API routes to enable dynamic sitemap generation

For now, document in HANDOFF.md: 'Remember to update public/sitemap.xml when adding routes'

findings.title: Could implement speakable schema for voice assistant optimization
findings.description: No speakable schema (speakable property in JSON-LD) is implemented. Speakable signals content suitable for voice assistants (Alexa, Google Assistant).

findings.title: No IndexNow integration
findings.description: No IndexNow API integration. IndexNow (Bing/Yandex) allows instant notification of new/updated pages to search engines, bypassing crawl queue.

findings.title: No about/mentions schema for E-E-A-T signals
findings.description: No about or mentions properties in Organization schema. These signal expertise, experience, authority, and trustworthiness (E-E-A-T) to AI models and search engines.

findings.title: Viewport meta tag not explicitly exported
findings.description: The root layout.tsx does not export a viewport configuration via the Metadata API or viewport export. While Next.js 13+ automatically adds a default viewport meta tag, it is best practice to explicitly configure it to ensure compatibility across all Next.js versions and to have control over device-width and scaling settings.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add viewport export to the root layout. In Next.js 14+, export a viewport constant: export const viewport: Viewport = { width: 'device-width', initialScale: 1, viewportFit: 'cover' };

findings.title: Missing meta descriptions on some static landing pages
findings.description: The Privacy Policy page has a minimal meta description ('Privacy policy for The New Beautiful Me.') that does not meet SEO best practices. Descriptions should be 120-160 characters and clearly communicate page content value to users.
findings.fix_suggestion: Expand descriptions to 120-160 characters with specific value proposition: 'Privacy policy for The New Beautiful Me. Learn how we protect your data, what information we collect, and your privacy rights. GDPR and CCPA compliant.'

findings.title: Static OG image used for all card pages instead of dynamic OG images
findings.description: All 78 card meaning pages, 3 guide pages, and 12 blog articles use the same static `/og.svg` image for Open Graph previews. The project has an OG image generation Lambda at `/api/og` that creates dynamic card images but it is not integrated into the card/guide page metadata.
findings.fix_suggestion: Generate dynamic OG images using the existing Lambda. For card pages: images: [{ url: `/api/og?type=card&name=${encodeURIComponent(card.name)}`, width: 1200, height: 630 }]

findings.title: Missing FAQ schema on FAQ-like content sections
findings.description: The /learn page has FAQPage schema (6 Q&As), but other pages with Q&A patterns don't use FAQPage schema. This content structure is perfect for FAQ markup which improves visibility in AI search (ChatGPT, Bing Chat, Google SGE) and voice assistant results.
findings.fix_suggestion: For blog articles with Q&A structure, add FAQPage schema with mainEntity array containing Question/Answer pairs.

findings.title: No image optimization for assets
findings.description: The application uses SVG logos and emoji symbols, which is good. However, PNG icon files could benefit from WebP/AVIF optimization, and no srcset/sizes attributes are on responsive images.
findings.fix_suggestion: Convert PNG icons to WebP format and serve with fallbacks. Consider adding preload directives for critical images.

findings.title: No speakable schema for voice search optimization
findings.description: The site has rich content about tarot that would benefit from speakable schema markup for voice assistants. No explicit properties are set for audio/voice optimization.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add speakable property to Article schema with xPath targeting key content sections for voice read-aloud.

findings.title: No IndexNow integration for faster indexing
findings.description: The site has 109+ auto-generated URLs. IndexNow could provide instant notification to search engines when content is added/updated, enabling faster crawling of new pages.
findings.fix_suggestion: Implement IndexNow submission in the build pipeline: POST to https://www.bing.com/indexnow with host, key, and urlList parameters.

findings.title: Minor link text improvements could enhance SEO
findings.description: Most link text is good, but some navigation labels could be more descriptive for both SEO and accessibility. For example, abbreviated labels in certain contexts could be expanded.
findings.fix_suggestion: Consider expanding abbreviated navigation labels where context is minimal. This is a low-priority enhancement.

findings.title: Twitter card on guides index page missing
findings.description: The guides index page exports metadata with OpenGraph but does not include twitter card configuration. All other major pages have proper Twitter card markup.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add twitter card configuration to guides/page.tsx metadata export with card: 'summary_large_image', title, and description.

findings.title: No explicit rel=preload for critical assets
findings.description: The application loads assets without explicit preload directives. While the app uses static export and inline Tailwind CSS, preload hints could slightly improve performance.
findings.fix_suggestion: If web fonts are added in future, include preload directives. Current system font approach is already optimized.

findings.title: Could enhance entity-based content structure for AI extraction
findings.description: The tarot card pages define entities well, but could add more explicit entity markup using schema.org Thing and sameAs properties to help AI models understand card relationships and synonyms.
findings.fix_suggestion: Add entity definition to Article schema with sameAs properties linking to external knowledge bases for card references.

findings.title: No AggregateOffer schema (future monetization preparation)
findings.description: The app currently offers free readings. While not needed now, adding AggregateOffer schema would prepare for future premium features and enable rich snippet display in search results.
findings.fix_suggestion: For future monetization, expand to AggregateOffer schema with lowPrice, highPrice, aggregateRating, and availability properties.
